name: Chicken Salad Imperial B1
recipeIngredient: 6 cups chicken broth or 3 lb can of chicken stock
1 head iceberg lettuce, cut into strips (¼ in by 2 in)
2 ¼ oz pickled scallions, tops and bottoms, cut into strips
2 ½ oz pickled red ginger, cut into long, thin strips
1 ½ oz Chinese pickles, cut into thin strips
2 ½ tbsp roasted and ground peanuts
2 ½ tbsp roasted and ground coconut
1/2 tsp powdered hot mustard mixed with 1/2 tsp water
1/4 lb wonton pastry, cut into long strips
recipeInstructions:
1. The day before, prepare the chicken broth using a standard recipe or use canned chicken stock for convenience. Slowly simmer until the meat is cooked through.
2. Place the chicken out overnight to dry.
3. The next day, deep fry in peanut oil until golden brown. Separate the meat from the bones and tear into long narrow strips with your hands.
4. Cut green onions, carrots, ginger, and pickles into long strips.
5. Combine green onions, lettuce, carrots, scallions, red ginger, pickles, peanuts, coconut, and sesame seeds in a bowl. Mix well.
6. Add the chicken to the mixture. Starting with the sugar, mix all other ingredients except parsley and wonton until thoroughly combined.
7. Just before serving, add parsley and wonton pastry and toss lightly. Serve cold.
